beats.activity: Wake up, stretch, and journal at home with coffee.
beats.reasoning: Maya is an early riser who loves sunrise journaling and avoids screens before noon; she naturally wakes around 6:30am to catch the morning light and center herself before the day.

beats.activity: Morning nature walk through Woodland Park, enjoying the early sunshine.
beats.reasoning: A short walk from home in her neighborhood aligns with her love of nature and mindful movement; she can still avoid screens and be back with time before work.

beats.activity: Commute by bus to Capitol Hill yoga studio.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed until 3pm, so Maya takes the bus; 40 minutes allows for the cross-town commute from Fremont to Capitol Hill during early morning.

beats.activity: Lead morning flow class at CorePower Yoga.
beats.reasoning: Yoga instructors typically teach morning classes; a 75-minute class is standard and fits her early-riser routine.

beats.activity: Break at studio: prep smoothie bowl and check schedule.
beats.reasoning: After teaching, Maya enjoys a smoothie bowl and stays screen-free until after 10am per her routine; she's still at the studio preparing for her next class.

beats.activity: Lead mid-morning vinyasa class at CorePower Yoga.; Lunch and admin time at studio: respond to emails and plan evening classes.; Lead afternoon power flow class.
beats.reasoning: Second teaching slot of the day; the USA vs Wales FIFA match at 11am is not relevant to Maya's work schedule, so she teaches through it.

beats.activity: Rest and hydrate at studio, journaling between classes.
beats.reasoning: Maya takes a mindful break to journal and recharge; she stays at the studio to prepare for evening teaching.

beats.activity: Lead evening yin yoga class at CorePower Yoga.
beats.reasoning: Yin classes are typically held in late afternoon; this 75-minute restorative session is perfect for her teaching style.

beats.activity: Commute home by bus, avoiding Light Rail closure (still in effect until 3pm, now resolved).
beats.reasoning: After her last class, Maya takes the bus back to Fremont; the Light Rail closure is now lifted, but she prefers the bus route she knows.

beats.activity: Evening nature walk through Green Lake, enjoying the sunny weather.
beats.reasoning: On a beautiful sunny day, Maya takes a detour to Green Lake for a restorative walk before heading home; it's a neighborhood close to Fremont and aligns with her love of nature.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat a light dinner at home.
beats.reasoning: Maya returns home to prepare a nourishing dinner; she keeps it light as she winds down for the evening.

beats.activity: Evening wind-down: read, gentle stretching, and meditation at home.
beats.reasoning: Maya maintains her calm evening routine with reading and gentle movement, staying screen-free to support good sleep hygiene.
Maya gets 8 hours of restful sleep, maintaining her early-riser routine and waking refreshed for tomorrow.

diary:
Woke up early with the sun streaming through my Fremont apartment—those first few minutes with coffee and my journal never get old. The walk through Woodland Park was exactly what I needed; the light was soft and golden, and I felt my body wake up before my mind caught up.

The studio was buzzing today. Morning flow went well, though I kept noticing students checking their phones around 11—something about the World Cup match, I think. I stayed focused on my own practice between classes, hydrating and journaling in the back room. By the time I taught yin in the afternoon, I was running on that good tired feeling.

After work, I took the long way home through Green Lake instead of dealing with the Light Rail mess. The water was glassy, and I just walked and breathed. Made a simple dinner, then spent the evening reading and stretching on my living room floor. Felt grounded.
thought_process: I structured my day around what my body needed—early movement, intentional teaching, recovery time. The World Cup buzz didn't really pull at me; I'm not much of a sports person, and honestly, I was too immersed in my classes to notice the hype. The Light Rail closure was a minor annoyance I'd mentally noted, but taking the bus worked fine, and it actually gave me permission to detour through Green Lake on the way home instead of rushing. My mood stayed pretty even all day—when you spend six hours teaching yoga, you kind of stay in that calm headspace. I chose rest over socializing tonight because I could feel my nervous system needed it after a full teaching schedule.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
